content.content:
1→# Habitat Skills — Agent Configuration
     2→
     3→This repository contains AI agent skills for founders building startups from scratch. Skills provide structured frameworks for ideation, validation, prototyping, and launch.
     4→
     5→## Skill Registry
     6→
     7→Each skill is a markdown file at `skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter.
     8→
     9→### Available Skills
    10→
    11→- `founder-context` — Set up project context (idea, audience, stage) that all other skills reference
    12→- `idea-validation` — Validate startup ideas using structured frameworks
    13→- `competitor-research` — Research competitors, map gaps, find positioning angles
    14→- `landing-page` — Build conversion-focused landing pages
    15→- `prototype-sprint` — Plan and execute focused prototype sprints
    16→- `mvp-scope` — Scope a true MVP from a feature wishlist
    17→- `first-users` — Find first 10-100 users with targeted playbooks
    18→- `launch-plan` — Create phased launch strategies
    19→
    20→## Skill Format
    21→
    22→### Required
    23→
    24→- `name`: 1-64 chars, lowercase a-z, numbers, hyphens. Must match directory name.
    25→- `description`: 1-1024 chars. Describe what it does and when to use it.
    26→
    27→### Optional
    28→
    29→- `version`: Semantic version (default: 1.0.0)
    30→- `license`: License identifier (default: MIT)
    31→
    32→### Structure
    33→
    34→```
    35→skills/
    36→  skill-name/
    37→    SKILL.md          # Main skill file (required)
    38→    references/       # Supporting reference files (optional)
    39→```
    40→
    41→## Conventions
    42→
    43→- Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines
    44→- Use H2/H3 headings and bullet points for scannability
    45→- Include trigger phrases in descriptions so agents can match intent
    46→- Reference related skills to help agents pick the right one
    47→- Check for `founder-context` before gathering info — avoid re-asking what's already known
    48→
    49→## Context File
    50→
    51→The `founder-context` skill creates `.claude/founder-context.md` in the user's project. All other skills should check for this file before asking questions.
    52→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→# Habitat Skills — AI Agent Skills for Founders
     2→
     3→A collection of AI agent skills that help aspiring founders go from idea to launched product. Built for [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) and compatible AI coding agents.
     4→
     5→From the team behind [Habitat](https://joinhabitat.eu) — where 101+ founders have launched prototypes in a single evening.
     6→
     7→## What are skills?
     8→
     9→Skills are markdown files that give AI agents specialized knowledge. Install them in your project, and your agent knows how to validate ideas, research competitors, scope MVPs, and build landing pages — using the same frameworks experienced founders use.
    10→
    11→## Available Skills
    12→
    13→### Phase 1: Ideation & Validation
    14→
    15→| Skill | Description |
    16→|-------|-------------|
    17→| [`founder-context`](skills/founder-context/SKILL.md) | Set up your project context so all other skills know your idea, audience, and stage |
    18→| [`idea-validation`](skills/idea-validation/SKILL.md) | Validate your idea with structured frameworks — TAM, problem-solution fit, Mom Test |
    19→| [`competitor-research`](skills/competitor-research/SKILL.md) | Research competitors, find gaps, and identify positioning angles |
    20→
    21→### Phase 2: Prototyping & Building
    22→
    23→| Skill | Description |
    24→|-------|-------------|
    25→| [`landing-page`](skills/landing-page/SKILL.md) | Build a conversion-focused landing page — copy, structure, and code |
    26→| [`prototype-sprint`](skills/prototype-sprint/SKILL.md) | Plan and execute a focused prototype sprint — scope, prioritize, build |
    27→| [`mvp-scope`](skills/mvp-scope/SKILL.md) | Cut your feature list down to a true MVP with a clear build checklist |
    28→
    29→### Phase 3: Launch & Traction
    30→
    31→| Skill | Description |
    32→|-------|-------------|
    33→| [`first-users`](skills/first-users/SKILL.md) | Find your first 10, then 100 users with a targeted outreach playbook |
    34→| [`launch-plan`](skills/launch-plan/SKILL.md) | Create a phased launch strategy — pre-launch, launch day, post-launch |
    35→
    36→## Installation
    37→
    38→### Quick Install (Recommended)
    39→
    40→```bash
    41→# Clone into your project
    42→git clone https://github.com/joinhabitat/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
    43→```
    44→
    45→### Manual Install
    46→
    47→1. Download the `skills/` folder
    48→2. Copy it to `.claude/skills/habitat/` in your project
    49→3. Skills will be available automatically
    50→
    51→### Git Submodule
    52→
    53→```bash
    54→git submodule add https://github.com/joinhabitat/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
    55→```
    56→
    57→## Usage
    58→
    59→Skills activate automatically when you ask relevant questions:
    60→
    61→```
    62→"Help me validate my startup idea"          → idea-validation
    63→"Research my competitors"                    → competitor-research
    64→"Build me a landing page"                   → landing-page
    65→"What should my MVP include?"               → mvp-scope
    66→"How do I find my first users?"             → first-users
    67→```
    68→
    69→Or invoke directly:
    70→
    71→```
    72→/idea-validation
    73→/competitor-research
    74→/landing-page
    75→```
    76→
    77→## Quick Start
    78→
    79→1. Install the skills
    80→2. Run `/founder-context` to set up your project context
    81→3. Start building — try `/idea-validation` to test your idea
    82→
    83→## Built by Habitat
    84→
    85→These skills were born from 23+ prototypes launched at [Habitat](https://joinhabitat.eu) evenings — where founders go from idea to shipped product in 5 hours.
    86→
    87→Want to build alongside 50+ others? [Join the next evening →](https://joinhabitat.eu)
    88→
    89→## Contributing
    90→
    91→We welcome contributions. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines.
    92→
    93→### Adding a new skill
    94→
    95→1. Create a folder in `skills/` with a `SKILL.md` file
    96→2. Follow the format of existing skills (YAML frontmatter + markdown)
    97→3. Keep files under 500 lines
    98→4. Submit a PR
    99→
   100→## License
   101→
   102→MIT — use these however you want.
   103→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→# Getting Started with Habitat Skills
     2→
     3→A step-by-step guide for non-technical founders. No coding experience needed.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## What are Habitat Skills?
     8→
     9→Habitat Skills are like cheat sheets for AI. When you install them, your AI assistant (Claude) learns how to help you with startup-specific tasks — validating ideas, researching competitors, building landing pages, and more.
    10→
    11→Think of it like hiring a startup advisor that lives inside your terminal.
    12→
    13→---
    14→
    15→## What You Need
    16→
    17→1. **A computer** (Mac, Windows, or Linux)
    18→2. **Claude Code** installed (free to start)
    19→3. **10 minutes** to set up
    20→
    21→That's it. No coding required.
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Step 1: Install Claude Code
    26→
    27→Claude Code is a terminal-based AI assistant made by Anthropic. Here's how to get it:
    28→
    29→### On Mac
    30→
    31→Open the **Terminal** app (search for "Terminal" in Spotlight — press Cmd + Space and type "Terminal").
    32→
    33→Then paste this and press Enter:
    34→
    35→```
    36→npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
    37→```
    38→
    39→**Don't have npm?** You need Node.js first. Go to [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org), download the installer, run it, then try the command above again.
    40→
    41→### First time?
    42→
    43→After installing, run:
    44→
    45→```
    46→claude
    47→```
    48→
    49→It will ask you to log in with your Anthropic account. Follow the prompts. Once you see the Claude prompt (`>`), you're in.
    50→
    51→---
    52→
    53→## Step 2: Create Your Project Folder
    54→
    55→In Terminal, type:
    56→
    57→```
    58→mkdir my-startup && cd my-startup
    59→```
    60→
    61→This creates a folder called "my-startup" and moves you into it. You can name it whatever you want.
    62→
    63→---
    64→
    65→## Step 3: Install Habitat Skills
    66→
    67→Still in Terminal, paste this:
    68→
    69→```
    70→git clone https://github.com/joinhabitat/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
    71→```
    72→
    73→This downloads all the Habitat Skills into your project. Done.
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## Step 4: Start Using the Skills
    78→
    79→Launch Claude Code inside your project:
    80→
    81→```
    82→claude
    83→```
    84→
    85→Now you can talk to Claude and it knows all the Habitat frameworks. Here's what to try:
    86→
    87→### Set Up Your Context (Do This First)
    88→
    89→Type:
    90→
    91→```
    92→Help me set up my founder context
    93→```
    94→
    95→Claude will ask you about your idea, your audience, and your goals. Answer conversationally — it's like chatting with a co-founder. This saves your info so every other skill knows your situation.
    96→
    97→### Validate Your Idea
    98→
    99→```
   100→Validate my startup idea
   101→```
   102→
   103→Claude will run your idea through a structured framework — problem severity, market size, riskiest assumptions — and give you a scorecard with next steps.
   104→
   105→### Research Competitors
   106→
   107→```
   108→Research my competitors
   109→```
   110→
   111→Claude will search for competitors, analyze their strengths and weaknesses, and help you find your positioning angle.
   112→
   113→### Build a Landing Page
   114→
   115→```
   116→Build me a landing page
   117→```
   118→
   119→Claude will write the copy AND the code for a landing page you can deploy tonight.
   120→
   121→### Scope Your MVP
   122→
   123→```
   124→What should my MVP include?
   125→```
   126→
   127→Claude will help you cut your feature list down to the absolute minimum that tests your core idea.
   128→
   129→### Find Your First Users
   130→
   131→```
   132→How do I find my first users?
   133→```
   134→
   135→Claude will create a playbook with specific outreach templates, channels, and a daily routine.
   136→
   137→### Plan Your Launch
   138→
   139→```
   140→Help me plan my launch
   141→```
   142→
   143→Claude will build a phased launch strategy — what to do before, during, and after launch day.
   144→
   145→---
   146→
   147→## The Habitat Evening Workflow
   148→
   149→If you're at a Habitat evening, here's the recommended order:
   150→
   151→| Time | What to Do | Command |
   152→|------|-----------|---------|
   153→| **6:00 PM** | Set up your context | "Help me set up my founder context" |
   154→| **6:15 PM** | Quick validation | "Validate my startup idea" |
   155→| **6:30 PM** | Scope what to build tonight | "Scope my MVP for tonight" |
   156→| **6:45 PM** | Start building | "Build me a landing page" or "Help me prototype this" |
   157→| **9:30 PM** | Polish and ship | "Help me deploy this" |
   158→| **10:00 PM** | Plan next steps | "How do I find my first users?" |
   159→
   160→---
   161→
   162→## Common Questions
   163→
   164→### "I don't know how to use the terminal"
   165→
   166→That's okay. Here's the absolute basics:
   167→
   168→- **Open Terminal:** On Mac, press Cmd + Space, type "Terminal", press Enter
   169→- **Type a command and press Enter** to run it
   170→- **Copy-paste works:** Cmd + V to paste into Terminal
   171→- That's all you need. Claude does the rest.
   172→
   173→### "I don't have an idea yet"
   174→
   175→Start with:
   176→
   177→```
   178→I don't have a specific idea yet. Help me brainstorm based on problems I've experienced.
   179→```
   180→
   181→Claude will walk you through it.
   182→
   183→### "I tried a command and got an error"
   184→
   185→Paste the error message to Claude:
   186→
   187→```
   188→I got this error: [paste the error here]. What do I do?
   189→```
   190→
   191→Claude can usually fix it for you.
   192→
   193→### "Can I use this without Claude Code?"
   194→
   195→The skills are markdown files — they work with any AI coding tool that supports them (Cursor, Codex, etc.). But Claude Code is the easiest way to get started.
   196→
   197→### "Do I need to pay for anything?"
   198→
   199→- **Claude Code:** Has a free tier. The Pro plan ($20/month) gives you more usage.
   200→- **Habitat Skills:** Free and open source, forever.
   201→
   202→---
   203→
   204→## Quick Reference Card
   205→
   206→Save this for the evening:
   207→
   208→| I want to... | Say this to Claude |
   209→|--------------|-------------------|
   210→| Set up my project | "Help me set up my founder context" |
   211→| Test my idea | "Validate my startup idea" |
   212→| Research competitors | "Research my competitors" |
   213→| Decide what to build | "Scope my MVP" |
   214→| Build a landing page | "Build me a landing page" |
   215→| Build a prototype | "Help me prototype this in 5 hours" |
   216→| Find users | "How do I find my first users?" |
   217→| Plan a launch | "Help me plan my launch" |
   218→| Get unstuck | "I'm stuck on [describe the problem]" |
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## Need Help?
   223→
   224→- **At a Habitat evening?** Raise your hand — mentors are here to help.
   225→- **Building on your own?** Ask in the Habitat community or open an issue on GitHub.
   226→- **Found a bug or have a suggestion?** [Open an issue](https://github.com/joinhabitat/habitat-skills/issues) on GitHub.
   227→
   228→Happy building.
   229→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

Based on the search results, here's information about Claude Code pricing, teams, and free tier limits:

## Pricing Plans

Claude offers Free/Pro/Max subscription tiers for individuals and small teams, with Pro at $20/month and Max at $100+/month.

The Team Plan costs $25 per person per month if billed annually or $30 per person per month if billed monthly, and requires a minimum of 5 members.

## Free Tier Limits

The Free tier offers about 40 short messages per day, but excludes access to the agentic Claude Code capabilities, and is best suited for casual experimentation, testing smaller snippets, or initial onboarding before adopting a paid plan.

The Free plan grants access to Claude Sonnet 4 with a recurring five-hour session that resets automatically, but usage limits vary depending on system load, message length, file size, and model chosen, and as global load increases, the number of free interactions per session can shrink.

## Team Features

Team plans provide access to Claude Code, projects, knowledge bases, and collaboration features, with a 200k context window. Team plans support up to 75 seats.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code skills sharing and collaboration with multiple people:

## Skills Sharing for Multiple People

Individual peer-to-peer skill sharing is not currently available — users cannot directly share skills with specific colleagues. For personal skills, each individual must upload them to their own account. However, organization Owners can provision skills for all users through Admin settings on Team and Enterprise plans.

## Broader Collaboration Features

Claude Code offers several collaboration mechanisms beyond skills:

**Project Sharing**: The primary collaboration feature is through shared project configuration where CLAUDE.md files, custom slash commands, and MCP configurations can be committed to version control, ensuring that all team members have access to the same Claude Code setup.

**Shared Workflows**: Team members can share custom slash commands and workflows that automate common team processes, building a shared library of efficient development patterns.

**Parallel Development**: Using Git worktrees, teams can run multiple Claude Code sessions on different branches or feature areas, enabling parallel development without conflicts.

**Agent Teams**: With Agent Teams enabled, a lead agent takes over your requirement, breaks it down, and spins up multiple teammates to work simultaneously. They don't just report to the lead; they communicate directly with each other and share findings.

Based on the search results, I found information about Claude Code authentication for teams and agent teams in 2026:

## Claude Code Authentication for Teams

If you're not seeing the option to authenticate with your preferred account in Claude Code, you can update it by logging out completely using the /logout command, running claude update, restarting your terminal, and then running claude to select the correct account.

Claude Code is included with every Team plan seat, with Premium seats offering more usage for team members with heavier workloads. For Enterprise plans, Claude Code is available on Chat + Claude Code seats (usage-based billing) and Premium seats (seat-based billing).

## Agent Teams Feature (2026)

In Claude Code, you can now assemble agent teams to work on tasks together. Agent teams have been introduced as a research preview, allowing you to spin up multiple agents that work in parallel as a team and coordinate autonomously—best for tasks that split into independent, read-heavy work like codebase reviews.

Agent teams are experimental and disabled by default, and can be enabled by adding CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS to your settings.json or environment.

Agent teams let you coordinate multiple Claude Code instances working together, with one session acting as the team lead to coordinate work, assign tasks, and synthesize results, while teammates work independently in their own context windows and communicate directly with each other.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
I've completed the research. Here's what I found:

## 1. Current User Documentation (GETTING-STARTED.md)

The guide tells users:
- Claude Code is "free to start" with a Pro plan ($20/month) available for more usage
- Habitat Skills are "free and open source, forever"
- Skills work with "any AI coding tool that supports them (Cursor, Codex, etc.)" but Claude Code is recommended as easiest
- Installation requires Anthropic account login: users run `claude`, which asks them to "log in with your Anthropic account"

**Tool Compatibility**: README.md mentions the skills are "compatible AI coding agents" and explicitly notes they work with tools like Cursor and Codex, but Claude Code is the primary platform.

## 2. Skills Files (README.md and AGENTS.md)

The skills documentation covers:
- **No authentication/access details** in README.md beyond the basic installation
- **AGENTS.md** is purely technical - it describes skill format (YAML frontmatter, structure) and conventions, with no information about authentication or multi-user access
- Skills are designed as markdown files that work with Claude Code or compatible agents
- No documentation about team sharing or multi-user scenarios

## 3. Web Research Findings

### Authentication & Team Access
- **Claude Code Team Plan** ($25-30/month per person, min 5 seats): Includes Claude Code with shared workspace features
- **Enterprise Plan**: Has Team or Premium seat options with Claude Code included
- **Authentication**: Users authenticate with their Anthropic account; Team/Enterprise plans support organizational management
- Organization owners can provision skills for all users through Admin settings

### Free Tier Limits (2026)
- **Free plan**: ~40 short messages per day, **excludes Claude Code access** (limited to chat)
- **Free plan quota**: Roughly 5-hour sessions that reset automatically
- **Limits vary** based on system load, message length, and file size

### Team/Multi-Person Features
- **Skills**: Organization owners can provision skills for all users; individual peer-to-peer sharing isn't available
- **Shared Projects**: Teams share CLAUDE.md files and custom commands via version control
- **Agent Teams** (2026 feature, experimental): Multiple Claude Code instances work in parallel on the same task, with one session as team lead coordinating work
- **Collaboration**: Primarily through shared Git repositories and project configurations, not direct skill sharing
